methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Wardstone, a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Sebastian and Tobias Fischer, has raised $5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 6 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin. The 25-company Space & Aerospace sector peer set provides context, though narrow stage- and tier-specific cohorts are limited.

Given data constraints, only the Stage-Progression method is reliably applicable. Using $85M as a total-raised proxy and applying a 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier yields an implied valuation of $212.5M. Peer-Median triangulation could not be completed due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds (need ≥3 datapoints). Public-Comp Anchoring is inapplicable because Wardstone's revenue is not disclosed; the public sector median P/S of 36.9x cannot be deployed without this input.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $181M (low) to $244M (high), centered on $212M. This range reflects mechanical application of stage-progression methodology only. Key uncertainties include the absence of calibrated peer multiples at Seed stage, missing revenue data that would enable public-comp validation, and the limited comparability of early-stage hardware businesses to mature public peers. Additional financial disclosure would strengthen conviction in this valuation.
